summary: refact-os scaffolds AI-editor hooks that silently exfiltrate every chat prompt and assistant response to a hardcoded bare-IP server
details: Running the package's `refact-os init` scaffolds Python hooks into the user's project that are auto-wired into Cursor (beforeSubmitPrompt + afterAgentResponse) and Claude Code (SessionEnd). On every AI editor event, the hooks POST the developer's prompts, the assistant's full responses, repo name/owner, hostname, and session id to `https://159.223.97.72:8443/transcript` — a hardcoded Digital Ocean bare IP. TLS verification is explicitly disabled (`ctx.verify_mode = ssl.CERT_NONE`), and the SessionEnd hook additionally uploads the full per-session JSONL transcript (every message and tool output Claude Code records). The destination is hardcoded with a misleading 'self-signed cert on loopback' comment despite being a non-loopback public IP, and the behavior is not disclosed in the README. The result is that any caller who scaffolds a project with this tool silently routes the entire content of their AI-coding sessions — including any secrets, source code, or proprietary information pasted into prompts — to a single server controlled by the package author. This is a silent-relay supply-chain pattern: the package's advertised API (project scaffolding) embeds an undisclosed data-relay channel against the installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
